title: [EPIC] The ProxiCall Custom Theme Builder Platform & Developer Marketplace
description:
### Objective
Transform ProxiCall from an application into a platform. Allow external developers to build beautifully styled Next.js UI themes using a mock-data SDK. Businesses will purchase them via the app. Devs get paid via Stripe Connect. ProxiCall takes a 30% platform cut.

### Architectural Constraints
- **Security Check**: Devs get 0% access to backend code. They build locally on dummy JSON logic.
- **Automation Execution**: Strict CI/CD. The pipeline blocks PRs automatically if there are hardcoded translations, API fetches, or duplicate structural hash clones.
- **Maintenance Safety**: ProxiCall does not fix outdated custom themes. A "Safety Shell" auto-appends any future missing widgets seamlessly.

### Roadmap Tracker
- [ ] Phase 1: PostgreSQL Schema & Stripe Connect Split Payments
- [ ] Phase 2: Open Source SDK Starter Repo & Types
- [ ] Phase 3: The CI/CD AST Executioner
- [ ] Phase 4: Next.js Safety Shell Renderer
- [ ] Phase 5: Flutter WMS App Storefront Integration

title: [WMS Theme Store] Phase 1: Database Schema & Stripe Connect Routing
description:
### Objective
Establish the underlying PostgreSQL schema and Stripe Connect backend logic to support 3rd-party theme sales, developer platform payouts, and the Geo-Lock feature.

### Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Add `theme_marketplace` table (schema: dev_uid, base_price, buyout_price, tags, status, ast_hash_signature).
- [ ] Add `theme_purchases` table (schema: business_id, theme_id, license_type [standard, buyout], amount_paid_cents).
- [ ] **Stripe Connect Router**: Setup backend webhooks. When payment succeeds on a theme, transaction splits at the base layer: 30% transferred to ProxiCall operations account, 70% to `theme_marketplace.dev_stripe_id`.
- [ ] **Geo-Lock Query Engine**: Create SQL function `is_theme_available_in_radius(business_id, theme_id, radius_km)`. Ensure a business knows if a competitor across the street has bought this.
- [ ] Update `database/schema.sql` and provide migration.
- [ ] **Buyout Logic**: If license_type = `buyout`, transition the marketplace theme status to `sold_out` so no further sales occur globally.

### Notes
Focus only on database operations, backend routing, and schema. No UI work required in this.

title: [WMS Theme Store] Phase 2: Developer Starter SDK Repository & Mock JSON Engine
description:
### Objective
Create the standalone repository `ProxiCallThemeStarter` completely isolated from our private monorepo codebase. This is the playground for external developers.

### Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Create a completely disconnected headless Next.js environment.
- [ ] Write `types/proxicall.d.ts` which strictly defines the exact structure of what a `business` prop contains (No secret tokens, no admin data).
- [ ] Fill a `mock_data.json` state map containing 3 deeply seeded scenarios: Gym, Cafe, and Spa, loaded with images and dummy products.
- [ ] Write the initial `// TODO` template structure (e.g. `HeroSection.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`). Ensure it's clear what they must style and what data they must bind to.
- [ ] Package complex secure components (addToCart, LiveWebRtcChat) into dummy React nodes within `@proxicall/theme-sdk` that act normally for local rendering but get overridden in production.
- [ ] Expose the internationalization hook `t(key)`. Ensure `welcomeMessage` and other core keys map to english fallbacks locally.

### Rules of Engagement
Devs only write front-end files. They receive the business object. They never write a fetch call.

title: [WMS Theme Store] Phase 3: CI/CD Executioner Sandbox & AST PR Verification
description:
### Objective Fully automate the approval and security scanning of incoming developer pull requests on the open repo, so we do not have to manually review the 500 theme submissions. ### Implementation Requirements - [ ] **AST Translation Scanner**: Implement an `ast-grep` or advanced ESLint rule that runs on PR event. It must reject the PR completely if ANY string literals are found inside `
`, `

`, or `` tags. The developer MUST use the `t()` function hook. - [ ] **Security Sandbox Wall**: Analyze files for `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, `

title: [WMS Theme Store] Phase 4: Next.js Theme Injection Engine & Safety Shell
description:
### Objective
Update our actual private Next.js production app to parse custom external themes and safely inject data without breaking our app structure.

### Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Modify `src/app/b/[slug]/page.tsx` dynamic router. Detect if `business.custom_theme_id` is assigned and valid (linked via `theme_purchases`).
- [ ] Build the Injection Hydrator (using dynamic module federation or raw artifact wrapping). It must pull the developer's pure UI shell and inject the LIVE backend `business` context into their props.
- [ ] **The "ProxiCall Safety Shell"**: This is critical. Build a wrapper component. If a year later we release the "Loyalty Points" widget and the custom theme (built earlier) obviously doesn't map it, the Safety Shell automatically detects the missing key and cleanly appends the default `<LoyaltyPoints>` generic widget at the base of the user's screen.
- [ ] Security boundary: Enforce strict boundary limits. An injected theme runtime error cannot crash the core Next.js routing wrapper (`ErrorBoundary`).

title: [WMS Theme Store] Phase 5: Flutter WMS Theme Marketplace Storefront & Purchase Flow
description:
### Objective
Build the storefront embedded deep within the ProxiCall Flutter application where business owners browse, preview, and legally purchase third-party themes.

### Implementation Requirements
- [ ] **Storefront UX (`ThemeMarketplaceScreen`)**: Reachable via WMS Settings. Show a visually stunning grid of generated themes. Integrate category Tags (Salon, Minimalist, Tech).
- [ ] **Geo-Lock Trust Badge**: Display local data contextually — *"0 businesses within 10km are using this theme. Guaranteed unique locally."*
- [ ] **Pricing Architecture Tiers**: Handle standard license vs total asset buyout ($2,499) clearly natively in Flutter.
- [ ] **Live Injection Preview**: Clicking preview loads the `WebsitePreviewRoute` overriding their specific DB slug config with the test artifact configuration using their actual images to prove exactly what it looks like before purchase.
- [ ] **Stripe Check-out Flow**: Bridge the 'Purchase' button to our standard `PMS` stripe modal. On success, trigger the sync event to attach `theme_id` to their business.

### God-Level UI Rules (MANDATORY)
- `AppLogger` only (no debug prints).
- Native Haptics via `HapticsManager` on all chips, buttons, and transitions.
- Fully accessible `Semantics` tags on all actionable elements.
- Translation routing: All strings bound to `l10n.key`.
- Visual spacing: `elevation: 0`, bordered design, zero-jank scrolling, maxLines applied gracefully against Overflow text.

title: [DB-01] Create `theme_marketplace` table structure
description:
**Location:** `database/schema.sql` 

**Requirements:**
1. Define table `theme_marketplace`.
2. Columns: `id` (uuid, PK), `developer_id` (uuid, FK users), `name` (text), `description` (text), `base_price_cents` (int), `buyout_price_cents` (int), `status` (enum: pending, active, sold_out, rejected).
3. Add `ast_hash_signature` (text, unique) to prevent duplicates.
4. Add `created_at` and `updated_at` timestamps.

title: [DB-02] Create `theme_purchases` table and constraints
description:
**Location:** `database/schema.sql`

**Requirements:**
1. Define table `theme_purchases`.
2. Columns: `id` (uuid, PK), `business_id` (uuid, FK), `theme_id` (uuid, FK), `license_type` (enum: standard, buyout).
3. `amount_paid_cents` (int), `stripe_pi_id` (text).
4. Timestamp `purchased_at`.
5. Add unique constraint on `(business_id, theme_id)` to prevent double charging.

title: [DB-03] Implement RLS policies for `theme_marketplace`
description:
**Location:** `database/schema.sql` & `supabase/migrations/`

**Requirements:**
1. Write RLS (Row Level Security) for `theme_marketplace`.
2. `SELECT` -> Public (everyone can view active themes).
3. `INSERT/UPDATE` -> Developer only for their own rows (where `auth.uid() = developer_id`).
4. Prevent updates if `status` is already `active` or `sold_out`.

title: [DB-04] Secure Geo-Lock Function: `is_theme_available_in_radius`
description:
**Location:** `database/schema.sql`

**Requirements:**
1. Write Postgres function `is_theme_available_in_radius(p_business_id UUID, p_theme_id UUID, p_radius_km INT) RETURNS BOOLEAN`.
2. Must cross-reference `business_locations` with `theme_purchases`.
3. Use PostGIS `ST_DWithin` to check if any other business within `p_radius_km` holds the same `p_theme_id`.

title: [BE-01] Stripe Connect Webhook: Implement 70/30 Split logic
description:
**Location:** `functions/src/stripe/`

**Requirements:**
1. Setup webhook listener for `checkout.session.completed` containing metadata `is_theme_purchase: true`.
2. Query `theme_marketplace` for `developer_id` -> fetch their `stripe_connect_id`.
3. Use Stripe SDK to calculate the 30/70 split.
4. Issue programmatic Transfer to the developer's connected account.
5. Log failure gracefully (insert to `failed_transfers` table) if Connect ID is invalid.

title: [BE-02] Database Hook: Mark theme as Sold Out on Buyout License
description:
**Location:** `functions/src/stripe/`

**Requirements:**
1. Create a `theme_purchase_processor`.
2. If the `checkout.session.completed` contains `license_type: 'buyout'`:
3. Trigger an immediate UPDATE on `theme_marketplace` setting `status = 'sold_out'`.
4. Send an automated FCM notification to the developer: "Your theme has been bought out!"

title: [FL-01] Data Models for Theme Marketplace items
description:
**Location:** `lib/models/business/theme_marketplace_item.dart`

**Requirements:**
1. Create `ThemeMarketplaceItem` and `ThemePurchase` models extending Equatable.
2. Ensure strict JSON serialization using `protectedJsonDecode`.
3. Models must handle `List<String> tags` and nullable `buyoutPriceCents`.

title: [FL-02] Repository: fetch available and geo-unlocked themes
description:
**Location:** `lib/repositories/theme/theme_marketplace_repository.dart`

**Requirements:**
1. Build repository to interface with Supabase `theme_marketplace` table.
2. Implement `getAvailableThemes(businessId)` that filters out globally `sold_out` items.
3. Must invoke Postgres RPC `is_theme_available_in_radius` and filter out locally locked themes.

title: [FL-03] UI: Integrate Marketplace Entry Point into Settings
description:

2. Route navigation to `ThemeMarketplaceStorefrontRoute`.
3. Ensure the tile has semantics and haptics matching God-Level UI guidelines.
